name: BAE Systems PLC
description: One of the world's largest defence and security companies, providing advanced technology-led solutions across air, maritime, land, cyber and space domains for military and government customers globally.

assumptions.revenueDrivers: Record order backlog of £77.8bn (funded and unfunded) underpins multi-year visibility. Sales grew 14% in FY2024 to £28.3bn driven by all segments. Ball Aerospace acquisition (Feb 2024, $5.5bn) added Space & Mission Systems. NATO defence spending increases, AUKUS submarine programme, GCAP fighter jet programme, and elevated global threat environment support sustained demand.
assumptions.marginDrivers: Return on sales stable at 10.6% in FY2024. Guidance for 2025: underlying EBIT growth 8-10%, sales growth 7-9%. Improving mix as higher-margin Electronic Systems grows. Ball Aerospace integration delivering cost synergies.
assumptions.risks: Elevated net debt at £4,945m post Ball Aerospace acquisition (FY2023: £1,022m). Geopolitical uncertainty, FX sensitivity ($1.28/GBP — 5c move impacts EBIT by c.£75m). Supply chain pressures in microelectronics. Large pension schemes. Regulatory and export licensing risks.
assumptions.catalyst: NATO defence budget increases above 2% GDP target. AUKUS submarine programme ramp-up. GCAP sixth-generation fighter programme. Strong free cash flow guidance (2025 >£1.1bn, depressed by Ball Aerospace integration). Share buyback programme (£1.5bn authorised 2023). DPS increased for 21st consecutive year.
